subaward_description: THE OBJECTIVE OF THIS EFFORTS IS TO:  1) PREPARE CLEAN SUBSTRATES FOR GROWTH OF SINGLE-LAYER REBAR GRAPHENE; 2) GROW REBAR GRAPHENE ON CLEAN SUBSTRATES, VARYING CONDITIONS TO OPTIMIZE AREA WITHOUT GRAIN BOUNDARIES; 3) VARY DENSITY OF REBAR BY CHANGING AMOUNTS, TYPES, DIAMETERS AND QUALITY OF CARBON NANOTUBES; 4) DEVELOP A METHOD TO SYSTEMATICALLY TUNE MORPHOLOGY, SUCH AS STACKING REBAR GRAPHENE SINGLE LAYERS TO MAKE 2 OR MORE LAYER REBAR GRAPHENE; 5) CONDUCT MICROTESTING OF SAMPLE MECHANICAL PROPERTIES; 6) CONDUCT NANOBULLET TESTING; AND 7) CONDUCT MICROTESTING BALLISTIC PROPERTIES, IF THEY ARE SUFFICIENTLY LARGE AND THICK.

subawardee_dba_name: TRUSTEES OF COLUMBIA UNIVERSITY IN THE CITY OF NEW YORK, THE

subaward_description: TO FULLY CHARACTERIZE THE ACTIVITY STATE OF THE HYDRA NERVE NET AND EVALUATE CONTROLLER PERFORMANCE WE WILL BUILD MICROFLUIDIC RECORDING CHAMBERS THAT CAN DELIVER CHEMICAL, ELECTRICAL, AND OPTICAL STIMULI WHILE PERFORMING TIME-LAPSE ELECTROPHYSIOLOGY AND CALCIUM IMAGING. MICROFLUIDIC DEVICES THAT WILL CONFINE HYDRA TO A QUASI-2D MEASUREMENT CHAMBER WILL BE FABRICATED BY CASTING PDMS OVER PHOTOLITHOGRAPHICALLY DEFINED SU8 MOLDS. PUSHDOWN VALVES WILL BE FABRICATED IN A SECOND PDMS LAYER AND PLASMA BONDED TO THE CHAMBER LAYER AS NEEDED. COMPLETED PDMS MOLDS WILL THEN BE BONDED TO 170 μM THICK GLASS SUBSTRATES SUCH THAT THE ENTIRE CHAMBER IS COMPATIBLE WITH INVERTED MICROSCOPY USING COVER-GLASS-CORRECTED OBJECTIVE LENSES. TO CONTROL THE PRECISE CONCENTRATIONS OF CHEMICAL STIMULI DELIVERED TO THE RECORDING CHAMBER WE WILL MIX THE DESIRED RATIO OF CHEMICAL AGONIST AND HYDRA BUFFER USING A COMPUTER CONTROLLED HYDROSTATIC PRESSURE REGULATOR AND ON-CHIP MICROFLUIDIC MIXING CHAMBER. ELECTROPHYSIOLOGY WILL BE PERFORMED USING A MICROFLUIDIC ACTUATED CARBON NANOTUBE FIBER (CNTF) ELECTRODE DEVELOPED IN THE PASQUALI LAB, AND MULTI-CHANNEL AMPLIFIERS (INTAN TECH). THE POSITION OF THE FIBERS WILL BE CONTROLLED TO WITHIN 10 UM BY FLOWING A HIGH-VISCOSITY SOLUTION (DEXTRAN IN HYDRA BUFFER SOLUTION) THROUGH THE FIBER CHANNEL AND ACTUATING VALVES THAT ALLOW THE FIBER TO UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF THE FLUID FLOW. WE WILL USE IMMOBILIZATION SUCTION PORTS TO HOLD THE HYDRA IN PLACE DURING RECORDINGS AS NEEDED. WE PLAN TO PERFORM ALL FABRICATION IN THE RICE UNIVERSITY CLEAN ROOM AND PERFORM VALIDATION OF SIMULTANEOUS ELECTROPHYSIOLOGY AND IMAGING USING SPINNING DISC CONFOCAL MICROSCOPY. WE PLAN AT LEAST TWO VERSIONS OF THESE DEVICES IN PHASE 1, WHERE IMMOBILIZATION AND ELECTRODE/FLUIDIC LAYOUTS ARE OPTIMIZED BASED ON THE RESULTS OF OUR 1ST GENERATION DEVICES. ONCE WE HAVE DEVELOPED ROBUST PROTOCOLS FOR SIMULTANEOUS IMAGING AND ELECTROPHYSIOLOGY, WE WILL DISTRIBUTE THE MICROFLUIDIC TESTBEDS TO THE YUSTE LAB FOR USE WITH HOLOGRAPHIC OPTICAL INTERROGATION.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: UNDERSTANDING CHILDHOOD HUNGER GRANTS - WAVE III  IGF::OT::IGF
prime_award_naics_description: ADMINISTRATIVE MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL MANAGEMENT CONSULTING SERVICES
subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
CONTEXTUALIZING CHILDHOOD FOOD SECURITY: DO NEIGHBORHOOD SOCIAL CHARACTERISTICS SHAPE THE RISK?
JUSTIN T. DENNEY, PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATOR, AND RACHEL TOLBERT KIMBRO, CO-PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATOR
BETTER UNDERSTANDING THE CONTRIBUTORS TO CHILDHOOD FOOD INSECURITY IS A PRIORITY OF RESEARCHERS AND POLICYMAKERS ALIKE (1, 2). PRIOR WORK HAS ESTABLISHED IMPORTANT INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY PREDICTORS OF FOOD INSECURITY BUT LARGELY FAILED TO ACCOUNT FOR LOCAL CONTEXT; AT THE SAME TIME THAT RATES OF FOOD INSECURITY HAVE INCREASED. THE PROPOSED RESEARCH AIMS TO EXAMINE THE RELEVANCE OF NEIGHBORHOOD CONTRIBUTORS TO FOOD INSECURITY AMONG CHILDREN, UTILIZING NATIONALLY-REPRESENTATIVE DATA. OUR RESULTS WILL ALLOW US TO CREATE INNOVATIVE RISK PROFILES WHICH ILLUMINATE HOW FAMILY-LEVEL AND NEIGHBORHOOD-LEVEL FACTORS COMBINE TO INFLUENCE FOOD INSECURITY. RESEARCH TO DATE HAS FOCUSED ON PROXIMATE CONDITIONS THAT CONTRIBUTE TO FOOD SECURITY AND RESULTING POLICIES HAVE BEEN AIMED AT ALLEVIATING FOOD INSECURITY BY CONCENTRATING ON HOUSEHOLD RESOURCES. THESE POLICIES, HOWEVER, HAVE STRUGGLED TO CURTAIL A GROWING PROBLEM AND ARE LIKELY TO FAIL IF COMMUNITY CHARACTERISTICS ARE NOT CONSIDERED IN THE ASSESSMENT OF CHILDHOOD FOOD SECURITY. SIGNIFICANT RESEARCH HAS DOCUMENTED THE IMPORTANCE OF THE ENVIRONMENTS IN WHICH WE LIVE FOR BOTH CHILD AND ADULT HEALTH BUT THIS EVIDENCE HAS YET TO BE APPLIED TO FOOD INSECURITY. WE AIM TO FILL THAT VOID BY UNDERTAKING THE FIRST SYSTEMATIC INVESTIGATION OF NEIGHBORHOOD INFLUENCES ON FOOD INSECURITY AMONG YOUNG CHILDREN IN THE UNITED STATES. WE BELIEVE THAT ILLUMINATING NEIGHBORHOOD CHARACTERISTICS WHICH ARE ASSOCIATED WITH FOOD INSECURITY WILL ALLOW BETTER IDENTIFICATION OF FOOD INSECURE CHILDREN IN ORDER TO TARGET INTERVENTIONS, WHICH WILL ASSIST FNS IN ITS GOAL OF ELIMINATING FOOD INSECURITY AMONG CHILDREN IN THE U.S. BY 2015.
USING UNIQUE, RESTRICTED-USE, NATIONALLY-REPRESENTATIVE LONGITUDINAL DATA, WE WILL ADDRESS THREE PRIMARY OBJECTIVES AND RELATED OVERARCHING HYPOTHESES:
OBJECTIVE 1: ESTABLISH THE ASSOCIATIONS BETWEEN NEIGHBORHOOD CHARACTERISTICS AND CHILDHOOD FOOD SECURITY AFTER ACCOUNTING FOR INDIVIDUAL-LEVEL DETERMINANTS OF RISK; AND ASSESS THE RELATIVE IMPORTANCE OF INDIVIDUAL, FAMILY, AND NEIGHBORHOOD-LEVEL FACTORS FOR THAT RISK.
HYPOTHESIS 1A: NEIGHBORHOOD SOCIAL AND ECONOMIC CHARACTERISTICS WILL INFLUENCE THE ODDS OF FOOD INSECURITY AMONG YOUNG CHILDREN INDEPENDENT OF INDIVIDUAL-LEVEL CHARACTERISTICS. FOR EXAMPLE, CHILDREN IN SOCIALLY AND ECONOMICALLY DISADVANTAGED NEIGHBORHOODS WILL EXPERIENCE HIGHER ODDS OF FOOD INSECURITY REGARDLESS OF THEIR FAMILIESÂ€™ SOCIOECONOMIC STANDING.
HYPOTHESIS 1B: NEIGHBORHOOD SOCIAL AND ECONOMIC CHARACTERISTICS WILL SHAPE THE DIRECTION OF, OR MODERATE, INDIVIDUAL-LEVEL MEASURES. FOR EXAMPLE, THE EFFECT OF HOUSEHOLD POVERTY STATUS ON CHILD FOOD INSECURITY WILL DEPEND ON NEIGHBORHOOD-LEVEL DISADVANTAGE. THIS WILL ILLUMINATE HOW SOME POOR CHILDREN AVOID FOOD INSECURITY WHILE OTHERS DO NOT.
OBJECTIVE 2: TEST WHETHER SOCIAL CAPITAL MODERATES THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN NEIGHBORHOOD CHARACTERISTICS AND FOOD INSECURITY.
HYPOTHESIS 2: SOCIAL CAPITAL MEASURED AT THE HOUSEHOLD LEVEL WILL SHAPE THE IMPACT OF NEIGHBORHOOD DISADVANTAGE ON CHILD FOOD INSECURITY. FOR EXAMPLE, THE IMPACT OF NEIGHBORHOOD DISADVANTAGE ON FOOD INSECURITY WILL BE LESS FOR CHILDREN IN HOUSEHOLDS REPORTING HIGHER SOCIAL CAPITAL.
OBJECTIVE 3: ASSESS THE INDIVIDUAL AND NEIGHBORHOOD CONDITIONS THAT ASSOCIATE WITH FOOD INSECURITY CHANGES FOR CHILDREN BETWEEN KINDERGARTEN AND THIRD GRADE. HYPOTHESIS 3: BETWEEN KINDERGARTEN AND THIRD GRADE, CHILDREN WITH HOUSEHOLD AND NEIGHBORHOOD DISADVANTAGES WILL PERSIST OR MOVE INTO FOOD INSECURITY AND CHILDREN WITH HOUSEHOLD AND NEIGHBORHOOD ADVANTAGES WILL PERSIST IN FOOD SECURITY OR MOVE OUT OF FOOD INSECURITY. METHODS: THE PIS FOR THIS PROPOSAL HAVE ACCESS TO RESTRICTED-USE, NATIONAL, LONGITUDINAL DATA THAT WILL ENABLE THE PROPOSED ANALYSES TO BEGIN RIGHT AWAY.

WE BELIEVE THAT ILLUMINATING NEIGHBORHOOD CHARACTERISTICS WHICH ARE ASSOCIATED WITH FOOD INSECURITY WILL ALLOW BETTER IDENTIFICATION OF FOOD INSECURE CHILDREN IN ORDER TO TARGET INTERVENTIONS, WHICH WILL ASSIST FNS IN ITS GOAL OF ELIMINATING FOOD INSECURITY AMONG CHILDREN IN THE U.S. BY 2015.
USING UNIQUE, RESTRICTED-USE, NATIONALLY-REPRESENTATIVE LONGITUDINAL DATA, WE WILL ADDRESS THREE PRIMARY OBJECTIVES AND RELATED OVERARCHING HYPOTHESES:
OBJECTIVE 1: ESTABLISH THE ASSOCIATIONS BETWEEN NEIGHBORHOOD CHARACTERISTICS AND CHILDHOOD FOOD SECURITY AFTER ACCOUNTING FOR INDIVIDUAL-LEVEL DETERMINANTS OF RISK; AND ASSESS THE RELATIVE IMPORTANCE OF INDIVIDUAL, FAMILY, AND NEIGHBORHOOD-LEVEL FACTORS FOR THAT RISK.
HYPOTHESIS 1A: NEIGHBORHOOD SOCIAL AND ECONOMIC CHARACTERISTICS WILL INFLUENCE THE ODDS OF FOOD INSECURITY AMONG YOUNG CHILDREN INDEPENDENT OF INDIVIDUAL-LEVEL CHARACTERISTICS. FOR EXAMPLE, CHILDREN IN SOCIALLY AND ECONOMICALLY DISADVANTAGED NEIGHBORHOODS WILL EXPERIENCE HIGHER ODDS OF FOOD INSECURITY REGARDLESS OF THEIR FAMILIESÂ€™ SOCIOECONOMIC STANDING.
HYPOTHESIS 1B: NEIGHBORHOOD SOCIAL AND ECONOMIC CHARACTERISTICS WILL SHAPE THE DIRECTION OF, OR MODERATE, INDIVIDUAL-LEVEL MEASURES. FOR EXAMPLE, THE EFFECT OF HOUSEHOLD POVERTY STATUS ON CHILD FOOD INSECURITY WILL DEPEND ON NEIGHBORHOOD-LEVEL DISADVANTAGE. THIS WILL ILLUMINATE HOW SOME POOR CHILDREN AVOID FOOD INSECURITY WHILE OTHERS DO NOT.
OBJECTIVE 2: TEST WHETHER SOCIAL CAPITAL MODERATES THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN NEIGHBORHOOD CHARACTERISTICS AND FOOD INSECURITY.
HYPOTHESIS 2: SOCIAL CAPITAL MEASURED AT THE HOUSEHOLD LEVEL WILL SHAPE THE IMPACT OF NEIGHBORHOOD DISADVANTAGE ON CHILD FOOD INSECURITY. FOR EXAMPLE, THE IMPACT OF NEIGHBORHOOD DISADVANTAGE ON FOOD INSECURITY WILL BE LESS FOR CHILDREN IN HOUSEHOLDS REPORTING HIGHER SOCIAL CAPITAL.
OBJECTIVE 3: ASSESS THE INDIVIDUAL AND NEIGHBORHOOD CONDITIONS THAT ASSOCIATE WITH FOOD INSECURITY CHANGES FOR CHILDREN BETWEEN KINDERGARTEN AND THIRD GRADE. HYPOTHESIS 3: BETWEEN KINDERGARTEN AND THIRD GRADE, CHILDREN WITH HOUSEHOLD AND NEIGHBORHOOD DISADVANTAGES WILL PERSIST OR MOVE INTO FOOD INSECURITY AND CHILDREN WITH HOUSEHOLD AND NEIGHBORHOOD ADVANTAGES WILL PERSIST IN FOOD SECURITY OR MOVE OUT OF FOOD INSECURITY. METHODS: THE PIS FOR THIS PROPOSAL HAVE ACCESS TO RESTRICTED-USE, NATIONAL, LONGITUDINAL DATA THAT WILL ENABLE THE PROPOSED ANALYSES TO BEGIN RIGHT AWAY.
